pages.text: $(italic)Map Atlases$() is the $(bold)other$() map in Novus, and it works nothing like the Antique Atlas. This one is built from real $(italic)cartographer's maps$() — the squared, top-down kind — and bundles them into a single book that fills itself in as you carry it.$(br2)Where the Antique Atlas draws biomes by feel, the Map Atlas shows the true grid: exact coordinates, exact coastlines, the maps you'd otherwise carry a chestful of, gathered into one.
pages.title: Map Atlases

pages.text: $(bold)Carry it and it grows.$() Hold the atlas and a $(italic)minimap$() appears in the corner of your screen; walk past the edge of what it knows and it quietly adds a new map tile to cover the ground. No crafting a map per region, no naming, no chest of paper.$(br2)Open the atlas item in hand for the full stitched view. You can place markers, and decorate the map with the usual banners and markers a cartography table allows.
pages.title: Carry It and It Grows

pages.text: $(bold)Two atlases, your choice.$() Both are in the pack on purpose. The $(italic)Antique Atlas$() (press $(bold)M$()) is the hand-drawn, explorer's-journal map bound to you; the $(italic)Map Atlas$() is the precise, coordinate-true map you hold in hand. Use the Antique Atlas to wander; reach for the Map Atlas when you want to know exactly $(italic)where$(). $(italic)Crafting is in EMI.$()
pages.title: Two Atlases
